Precision glass for advanced displays to deliver richer experiences
Auto glass and ceramics to drive cleaner, safer, and smarter transportation
Solar polysilicon, wafers, and innovative photovoltaic modules, enabling low-cost solar energy solutions

Role Purpose

On a daily basis the QA Analyst will collect, clean, and analyze data from multiple sources to generate meaningful insights that support business decision-making. Responsibilities include developing reports, dashboards, and visualizations, monitoring key performance indicators (KPIs), identifying trends and patterns, and performing statistical analysis to address business needs. The position also works closely with cross-functional teams to understand requirements, maintains databases and related documentation, presents findings and recommendations to management and stakeholders, ensures data accuracy and integrity, and completes other administrative duties as assigned.

Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze data from multiple sources
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations to support business decisions
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and measure business performance
Identify trends, patterns, and insights from data
Work with cross-functional teams to understand business requirements
Create and maintain databases, data systems, and documentation
Perform statistical analysis to solve business problems
Present findings and recommendations to management and stakeholders
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency
Completes other administrative duties as assigned

Experiences/Education - Required

Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Business, or a related field
1-2 years Proven experience as a QA Analyst, Software Tester, or in a similar quality assurance role
Experience with test case design, defect tracking, and reporting
Familiarity with tools such as PowerBI, Share Point, and Microsoft office
Strong analytical, troubleshooting, and problem-solving skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
High attention to detail and commitment to quality

This position does not support immigration sponsorship.

The range for this position is $67,237.00 - $92,451.00 assuming full time status. Starting pay for the successful applicant is dependent on a variety of job-related factors, including but not limited to geographic location, market demands, experience, training, and education.

A job that shapes a life.

Getting paid for our work is important, but feeling appreciated and recognized for those contributions motivates us much more. That’s why Corning offers a recognition program to celebrate successes and reward colleagues who make exceptional contributions.

new_jobs.description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world’s most complex infrastructure challenges. As a Project Coordinator
supporting our Carolinas Aviation team
, you'll play a key role in helping deliver capital improvement programs and aviation infrastructure projects that connect communities and drive long-term growth.

In this role, you'll support project managers and multidisciplinary teams by coordinating schedules, tracking budgets, maintaining project documentation, and supporting public grant compliance requirements. You'll help ensure projects are delivered efficiently by managing project information, facilitating stakeholder communication, monitoring contract commitments, and providing administrative and project delivery support throughout the project lifecycle.

What You’ll Do

Support capital program and project delivery activities, including budget tracking, grant compliance, reimbursement requests, and reporting requirements
Assist project managers with project planning, contracting, and delivery
Coordinate with internal teams, subcontractors, clients, funding agencies, and other external stakeholders
Track project schedules, milestones, deliverables, and deadlines across multiple concurrent projects and communicate with team members to help keep projects on schedule
Prepare and coordinate monthly, quarterly, and ad hoc project reports
Manage project invoicing, contract documentation, and scope tracking to support successful project delivery
Maintain accurate project records, including correspondence, decisions, approvals, file transfers, and project documentation
Coordinate meetings, distribute project information, and facilitate communication among project teams and stakeholders
Review project documents, deliverables, and communications for completeness, consistency, and accuracy
Support public funding and grant administration requirements, including documentation and compliance reporting
Collaborate with construction management, design, environmental, and permitting teams to support project objectives

Here's what you'll need

Bachelor's degree in Business, Project Management, Construction Management, Engineering, Planning, or a related field; experience in lieu of a degree will be considered
5+ years of experience supporting infrastructure, transportation, construction, engineering, capital improvement projects, or equivalent fields
Experience coordinating project schedules, budgets, documentation, and reporting activities
Experience supporting contract administration, invoicing, and project financial tracking
Experience working with diverse stakeholder groups, including clients, consultants, contractors, and regulatory agencies
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, including Excel, Word, Outlook, and Teams
Ability to maintain accurate records and manage complex project documentation

Ideally, you’ll also have

Experience supporting aviation, transportation, or other public infrastructure programs
Experience with federally or publicly funded projects and grant compliance requirements
Knowledge of project delivery processes, construction administration, or capital program management
Familiarity with project management, scheduling, or document control software
Experience supporting multidisciplinary engineering or construction teams
Understanding of environmental permitting, right-of-way, land acquisition, or construction project coordination activities
Experience in a client-facing project support or assistant project management role

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.
